title: Global Open Source Collaboration in Action
content:
OpenForum Europe and the UN Open Source Week 2025

UN launched the UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report at OpenForum Europe’s EU Open Source Policy Summit 2026. This marks the fourth collaboration between the UN and OFE. With the 2026 edition approaching on 22–26 June 2026, revisiting the 2025 conference report provides valuable insights, inspiration, and a clear sense of what to expect.

Omar Mohsine (Open Source Coordinator,

title: Join the OFE Team as a Policy Intern
content:
Deadline for application: 20 March 2026

Interviews will start immediately and continue until the vacancy has been filled

Start date: as soon as possible, exact date to be agreed

Contract: Internship based on a convention d’immersion professionnelle, i.e. CIP contract, with a monthly €1200 allowance

Duration: 6 months

Location: Brussels

About OpenForum Europe

OpenForum Europe (OFE) is a not-for-profit think tank that promotes openness in computing to policymakers and communities across Europe. Our Policy Research and Development team in Brussels works with a network of partners, supporters, academics, and specialist advisors. OFE focuses on key digital policy areas, including open technologies, standardisation, cybersecurity, digital government, competitiveness and innovation policy, digital sovereignty, public procurement, copyright, cloud computing, digital platforms, and internet policy. Our work is guided by the principles of user-centricity, compet

title: The EU Open Source Policy Summit 2026: Key learnings and takeaways
content:
The EU Open Source Policy Summit 2026, held on 30 January in Brussels under the theme ‘Digital Sovereignty Runs on Open Source’, brought together European policymakers, industry leaders, and open source advocates to address how open technologies can deliver on Europe’s competitiveness and sovereignty ambitions as part of the EU Open Source Week. This year’s Summit also announced a key transition: the appointment of Laszlo Igneczi as OpenForum Europe‘s new Executive Director.

The event was well-timed, given the announcement in early January of a new Call for Evidence on the topic of Open Digital Ecosystems, which will inform the development of the European Commission’s new open source software strat
snippet: The EU Open Source Policy Summit 2026, held on 30 January in Brussels under the theme ‘Digital Sovereignty Runs on Open Source’, brought together European policymakers, industry leaders, and open sour
creator: Nicholas Gates
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe

title: Connecting Europe’s Open Source Community: EU Open Source Week 2026
content:
Every year, at the end of January, the global open source and open technology community descends on Brussels, on the occasion of the FOSDEM conference. Since 2025, the EU Open Source Week has grown, including OpenForum Europe’s (OFE) flagship event: The EU Open Source Policy Summit, offering a unique opportunity to connect with policymakers, community members, businesses, civil society and developers.

OFE is proud to have been involved in many activities throughout the EU Open Source Week 2026, which featured over 35 events on the official EU Open Source Week platform. To make the week more connected than ever, OFE also launched a Networking Platform, which highlighted all events, increased their visibility, and facilitated connections among many participants.

Now that the EU Open Source Week 2026 has ended, we reflect on a week full of events and activities.

creator: Clara Thébert

title: Open Technology Research Officially Launches to Strengthen Global Research Community
content:
Brussels, February 2026 – OpenForum Europe (OFE), the Open Source Initiative (OSI), and the Open Knowledge Foundation (OKFN) announced last Friday, 30 January at the EU Open Source Policy Summit the official launch of Open Technology Research (OTR) — a strategic partnership designed to strengthen the global open technology research community and advance evidence-based policymaking around open technologies.

title: OpenForum Europe Appoints Laszlo Igneczi as Executive Director
content:
OFE Open Source Policy Summit 2026, Brussels – 31 January 2026

OpenForum Europe (OFE) is pleased to announce the appointment of Laszlo Igneczi as its new Executive Director. The announcement marks the start of a new chapter for the organisation as it enters its twenty-fourth year of championing the transformative power of open technologies.

Laszlo brings three decades of experience spanning EU institutions, national ministries and the private sector to OFE. His career has combined in-depth policy work, liaising with EU institutions and private actors, organisational leadership, and operational execution, always focusing on the next step in technology and crafting a regulatory response to harness the opportunities ahead.

Most recently, he served for ten years as Director of the BEREC Office, the EU Agency supporting the Body of European Regulators for Electronic Communications. In that role, he led the agency through a period of significant change, enabling BEREC to extend its focus from traditional telecoms regulation toward emerging challenges in the data economy, artificial intelligence, and the shift to from hardware-centric networks to
snippet: OFE Open Source Policy Summit 2026, Brussels – 31 January 2026 OpenForum Europe (OFE) is pleased to announce the appointment of Laszlo Igneczi as its new Executive Director. The announcement marks the
creator: Sivan Pätsch
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe

title: Open technologies, public procurement and economic impact: lessons from Denmark for Europe’s next digital laws
content:
Europe’s debate on cloud, artificial intelligence and digital sovereignty often assumes a trade-off between competitiveness, control, and cost. Evidence from public-sector procurement suggests the opposite. When governments use their purchasing power to create sustained demand for open source technologies, supported by appropriate institutional capacity, they can strengthen domestic digital ecosystems and economic growth, improve procurement outcomes and reduce strategic dependency at the same time.

The Danish government’s recent report on the use of open technologies in the public sector arrives at a critical moment for European digital policy. As the EU prepares the Cloud & AI Development Act and the upcoming revision of the Public Procurement Directive, the report provides a grounded account of how open technologies are used in practice, what barriers remain, and which institutional arrangements enable public administrations to act more strategically.

For OpenForum Europe, the Danish analysis reinforces a long-standing o

creator: Astor Nummelin Carlberg
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe

title: 2025 in Review: Twenty-Three Years of Open Technology Dialogue and Policy Engagement
content:
Dear friends and partners,

As I look back on 2025, my final year as Executive Director of OpenForum Europe, I want to extend my sincere thanks to all who have shaped this organisation. I am grateful to the OFE staff, past and present, for their commitment to our mission, and to the OFE Board for their steady support over the years. I also want to acknowledge our wider network of supporters and partners. Any progress we have achieved has been the result of collective effort and the goodwill that defines this community.

As OFE marks twenty-three years, it is clear that interest in open technologies has risen and receded across different phases of Europe’s digital agenda. What distinguishes the present moment is the recognition that competitiveness and digital sovereignty require more than general principles. Open technologies and open innovation have become practical instruments for addressing these challenges, and the policy debate reflects a deeper understanding of their strategic value.

OFE’s contribution has always depended on its community, and the organisation’s role will remain grounded in that shared purpose as it enters its next chapter.

creator: Astor Nummelin Carlberg
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe

title: OFE, OSI & OKF Form Partnership to Advance Open Technology Research
content:
Rio de Janeiro, November 2025 – OpenForum Europe (OFE), the Open Source Initiative (OSI), and the Open Knowledge Foundation (OKFN) today announced a strategic partnership — the Open Technology Research Network (OTRN) — to strengthen the global open technology research community and advance evidence-based policymaking around open technologies.

Open technologies — including software, data, and standards — underpin today’s digital infrastructure, powering innovation globally across business, government, and society at large. Yet despite their ubiquity, there remains a significant gap in understanding their full social, political, and economic impact. The absence of robust, coordinated research limits the ability of societies around the world to realise the full value of this immense source of innovation and to apply it to pressing policy challenges — from digital sovereignty and innovation policy to industrial competitiveness and economic growth.

To address this challenge, the partnership will focus on three key areas: (1) joint collaboration around a shared research agenda, (2) the development of the Open Technology Rese
snippet: Rio de Janeiro, November 2025 – OpenForum Europe (OFE), the Open Source Initiative (OSI), and the Open Knowledge Foundation (OKFN) today announced a strategic partnership — the Open Technology Researc
creator: Nicholas Gates
sourceDescription: Open Technologies for a Competitive and Sovereign Europe
